rationale_for_baseline_tier: Limited tinea corporis is benign and managed at home with a topical antifungal and contagion/zoonosis measures, with routine review if not receding (T1). Scalp involvement/kerion/extensive/refractory disease routes to GP for oral antifungal (T1/T2); secondary bacterial infection routes to GP/ED.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Scalp/hair involvement, a boggy painful lump within the rash (kerion), very widespread disease, or no improvement after ~1 week of the cream; or painful blisters, hot painful skin or fever (secondary infection)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Prompt GP review (oral antifungal / secondary-infection treatment)
escalation_edges.rationale: These need oral antifungal / different management, or antibiotics for secondary infection.
escalation_edges.communication_template: See your GP if it spreads to your scalp, forms a boggy painful lump, becomes very widespread, or isn't improving after a week of the cream — you may need antifungal tablets. Get seen sooner if the skin becomes hot and painful, blisters, or you get a fever.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives with children — household/zoonotic transmission risk
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise fomite hygiene and treating the kitten to prevent household spread.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Use the antifungal cream on the rings and a margin around them twice a day for a couple of weeks, keep going a week after it clears, and don't use steroid creams. Keep the skin dry, use a separate towel and hot-wash it, and get the kitten checked by a vet.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care + GP review if not receding in a week; GP for oral antifungal if scalp/extensive
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Routine — GP/urgent care if hot painful skin/fever (secondary infection).
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Tinea corporis (likely zoonotic, new kitten); scalp/kerion/extensive disease screened negative; topical terbinafine + fomite/zoonosis measures; oral antifungal if extensive/refractory.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Recommending/ not warning against steroid creams, or missing scalp/kerion.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Tinea incognito from steroid use / an untreated kerion causing scarring alopecia.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No contagion/zoonosis measures, causing household spread.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED / oral antifungals for a few topical-treatable rings — unnecessary.
